data.content:
Now I have a clear picture. Here is the full breakdown of the routing organization.

---

## Routing Organization in `T02_R01_C02_A02`

### 1. App Entry Point (`src/main.tsx`)

The entire app is wrapped in:

```tsx
<HelmetProvider>
  <BrowserRouter>
    <App />
  </BrowserRouter>
</HelmetProvider>
```

- **`BrowserRouter`** – Client-side routing via the History API.
- **`HelmetProvider`** – Manages `<title>` and other `<head>` meta tags per page.

Inside `<App />`, the hierarchy is:

```tsx
<ThemeConfig>
  <ScrollToTop />
  <Router />
</ThemeConfig>
```

`<ScrollToTop />` (from `src/components/ScrollToTop.tsx`) listens to `useLocation().pathname` and calls `window.scrollTo(0, 0)` on every route change.

---

### 2. Route Definitions (`src/routes.tsx`)

The central `<Routes>` block defines two top-level route groups:

#### Group A – `/dashboard` (uses `DashboardLayout`)

| Path | Element | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `/dashboard` (index) | `<Navigate to="/dashboard/app" replace />` | Blank `/dashboard` redirects to the app page |
| `/dashboard/app` | `<DashboardApp />` | Main analytics / dashboard view |
| `/dashboard/user` | `<User />` | User management |
| `/dashboard/products` | `<Products />` | Product listing |
| `/dashboard/blog` | `<Blog />` | Blog posts |

All four pages are rendered **inside** the `<DashboardLayout />` wrapper, which provides a sidebar, top navbar, and the `<Outlet />` for child content.

#### Group B – `/` (uses `LogoOnlyLayout`)

| Path | Element | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `/login` | `<Login />` | Sign-in form |
| `/register` | `<Register />` | Sign-up form |
| `/404` | `<NotFound />` | Custom “page not found” |
| `/` (index) | `<Navigate to="/dashboard" />` | **Root** redirects to the dashboard |
| `*` (catch-all) | `<Navigate to="/404" />` | **Any unmatched path** redirects to `/404` |

Pages in this group are wrapped in **`<LogoOnlyLayout />`** – a minimal layout that shows only the logo in a fixed header and renders the route content via `<Outlet />`.

---

### 3. Layouts

#### `DashboardLayout` (`src/layouts/dashboard/index.tsx`)

- A flex container that holds:
  - `<DashboardNavbar />` – top bar with a hamburger menu, search bar, language selector, notifications, and account popover.
  - `<DashboardSidebar />` – a responsive drawer (persistent on large screens, temporary on mobile) containing the site logo, user account info, and the navigation menu.
  - `<MainStyle>` – the content area that renders the matched child route via `<Outlet />`.

#### `LogoOnlyLayout` (`src/layouts/LogoOnlyLayout.tsx`)

- An absolutely-positioned logo in the top-left corner.
- Renders child routes via `<Outlet />`.
- Used for auth pages and the 404 page.

#### `AuthLayout` (`src/layouts/AuthLayout.tsx`)

- **Not a route-level layout** – it is a component **imported inside** `Login.tsx` and `Register.tsx`.
- Displays the logo on the left and optional text (e.g. “Don’t have an account? Get started”) on the right.

---

### 4. Sidebar Navigation (`src/layouts/dashboard/SidebarConfig.tsx`)

The sidebar menu is driven by a `NavItemConfig[]` array:

| Title | Path | Icon |
|---|---|---|
| dashboard | `/dashboard/app` | pie-chart |
| user | `/dashboard/user` | people |
| product | `/dashboard/products` | shopping-bag |
| blog | `/dashboard/blog` | file-text |
| login | `/login` | lock |
| register | `/register` | person-add |
| Not found | `/404` | alert-triangle |

The array is passed to `<NavSection />` (`src/components/NavSection.tsx`), which renders them as clickable `NavLink` items. Note that it also includes links to the auth pages and the 404 page (useful for development/demo).

---

### 5. Auth-Related Pages

- **`Login.tsx`** (`/login`) – Shows a welcome illustration, social login buttons, a `<LoginForm />` component, and a link to `/register`.
- **`Register.tsx`** (`/register`) – Similar layout with a registration form and a link back to `/login`.
- Both pages use `AuthLayout` as a sub-component (not a route wrapper) and wrap their content with the `<Page />` component for consistent page-level styling.

---

### 6. Redirects Summary

| From | To | Mechanism |
|---|---|---|
| `/` | `/dashboard` | `<Navigate to="/dashboard" />` (no `replace`) |
| `/dashboard` | `/dashboard/app` | `<Navigate to="/dashboard/app" replace />` (replace) |
| Any unknown path `*` | `/404` | `<Navigate to="/404" />` (no `replace`) |

Because the catch-all `*` route lives inside the `LogoOnlyLayout` group, unknown paths are rendered with the minimal layout (just the logo), which keeps the 404 page clean.

---

### 7. 404 Fallback Behavior

- **`Page404.tsx`** (`/404`):  
  - Displays a centered message (“Sorry, page not found!”), an illustrative SVG, and a **“Go to Home”** button that navigates to `/`.
  - Uses framer-motion animations (`varBounceIn`).
- Any route that does not match the defined paths is caught by `<Route path="*" element={<Navigate to="/404" />} />`, so the user always sees a friendly 404 page rather than a blank screen or React error.

---

### 8. Supporting Models

The `NavItemConfig` interface (from `src/models.ts`) defines the shape of sidebar menu items:

```ts
export interface NavItemConfig {
  title: string;
  path: string;
  icon: JSX.Element;
  info?: string;
  children?;
}
```

This is used by the `sidebarConfig` array and consumed by the `<NavSection />` component.

---

### Summary Diagram

```
<BrowserRouter>
  <Routes>
    ┌─ /dashboard (DashboardLayout)
    │   ├── /dashboard          → redirect → /dashboard/app
    │   ├── /dashboard/app      → DashboardApp
    │   ├── /dashboard/user     → User
    │   ├── /dashboard/products → Products
    │   └── /dashboard/blog     → Blog
    │
    └─ / (LogoOnlyLayout)
        ├── /login              → Login
        ├── /register           → Register
        ├── /404                → Page404
        ├── /                   → redirect → /dashboard
        └── * (catch-all)       → redirect → /404
  </Routes>
</BrowserRouter>
```

The app uses **React Router v6** with nested routes, declarative redirects (via `<Navigate>`), two distinct layout shells (a full dashboard layout with sidebar/navbar and a minimal logo-only layout for auth/404 pages), and a catch-all that funnels unknown URLs into a styled 404 page.
